title: Crumbly Cozy Japanese Masarepa Onigiri
description: A fresh and crumbly Japanese dish from the Shikoku region, featuring yellow masarepa, traditionally prepared with a mortar and pestle, and formed into onigiri shapes, suitable for a Buddhist temple meal.
instructions: Pound the yellow masarepa in a mortar with a pestle until it is well broken down and fragrant.
Gradually add in the water, pounding and stirring with the pestle until a crumbly dough forms.
Add the salt, sesame seeds, rice vinegar, sugar, and kombu powder to the dough and mix until just combined.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a triangle.
Brush the tops of the onigiri with grilled sesame oil.
Serve the onigiri at room temperature or slightly warmed.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
